# EU PEERS — European Practitioners for Integrated Home Renovation Services

> EU PEERS is an EU LIFE-funded project (LIFE22-CET-EU-Peers/101120790) that supports the deployment and scaling of One-Stop Shops (OSS) for integrated home energy renovation across Europe. The project runs a Community of Practice connecting public authorities, energy agencies, renovation programme managers, and service providers working on residential building renovation.

## What this site covers

EU PEERS addresses the practical challenge of setting up and operating One-Stop Shops — single points of contact that guide homeowners through the full renovation journey: energy audit, works specification, contractor coordination, financing, and quality assurance. The site serves as both the public-facing platform for the project and a knowledge-sharing hub for practitioners.

Key subject areas:
- One-Stop Shop design, governance, and business models (public, private, hybrid)
- Financing instruments for residential energy renovation
- Homeowner activation and engagement strategies
- Monitoring and impact assessment of renovation programmes
- Energy poverty and renovation access
- Multilevel governance in building renovation policy
- Quality assurance in renovation works
- Alliances and partnerships for OSS deployment

## Questions this site can help answer

- What is a One-Stop Shop for home energy renovation, and how does it work?
- How do I set up a One-Stop Shop in my city or region?
- What business models exist for One-Stop Shops — public, private, or hybrid?
- How are One-Stop Shops financed, and what financing instruments are suitable for residential renovation?
- How do One-Stop Shops engage and activate homeowners to renovate?
- How do you measure the impact of a One-Stop Shop?
- What governance structures work for One-Stop Shops at municipal, regional, and national levels?
- How can One-Stop Shops address energy poverty?
- What quality assurance frameworks exist for renovation works coordinated through an OSS?
- What partnerships do One-Stop Shops need with local authorities, banks, and contractors?
- Which One-Stop Shops are operating in Europe, and what can we learn from them?
- What is integrated home renovation, and how does it differ from standard energy efficiency measures?
- What EU policies and funding support the deployment of One-Stop Shops?
- How do I join the EU PEERS Community of Practice?

## Site structure

### Core pages
- [Home](https://eu-peers.eu/)
- [About Us](https://eu-peers.eu/about-us) — project overview, consortium, funding context
- [Our Mission](https://eu-peers.eu/our-mission) — goals and approach
- [How to Set Up a One-Stop Shop](https://eu-peers.eu/how-to-setup-an-one-stop-shop) — practical guidance for OSS establishment

### Knowledge and learning
- [Knowledge Hub](https://eu-peers.eu/knowledge-hub) — curated resources, guides, research, and reports on OSS topics; organised by asset type and category
- [Learning Lab](https://eu-peers.eu/learning-lab-v-1) — interactive online workshop series covering specific OSS topics monthly, led by different consortium partners
- [Glossary](https://eu-peers.eu/glossary) — terminology definitions for the OSS and renovation sector
- [FAQs](https://eu-peers.eu/faqs) — frequently asked questions about integrated home renovation services and the EU PEERS project

### Cases and showcases
- [Cases](https://eu-peers.eu/success-cases) — documented examples of One-Stop Shops operating across Europe, with data on their models, countries, and operational approaches

### News, events, and media
- [News & Events](https://eu-peers.eu/news-and-events) — project updates and announcements
- [News](https://eu-peers.eu/news) — articles and publications
- [Events](https://eu-peers.eu/events) — upcoming events
- [Past Events](https://eu-peers.eu/past-events) — archive of completed events with post-event reports
- [Media Center](https://eu-peers.eu/media-center) — press releases and brand assets

### Community
- [Community Life](https://eu-peers.eu/community-life) — overview of national and transnational Communities of Practice
- [Members](https://eu-peers.eu/members) — directory of member organisations (229+ across Europe)
- [Member Benefits](https://eu-peers.eu/member-benefits) — what membership offers
- [Member Application](https://eu-peers.eu/membership-application) — join the community

### Campaign
- [Cities & Regions Campaign](https://eu-peers.eu/cities-and-regions-campaign) — initiative mobilising local authorities to support OSS deployment

### National and regional Communities of Practice

EU PEERS operates national Communities of Practice where OSS practitioners exchange knowledge in their own languages and contexts:

- [Spanish Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/spain) — also available in [Spanish](https://eu-peers.eu/spain-es)
- [Italian Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/italy) — also available in [Italian](https://eu-peers.eu/italy-it)
- [Hungarian Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/hungary) — also available in [Hungarian](https://eu-peers.eu/hungary-hu)
- [Irish Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/ireland)
- [Baltic Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/baltics) — covering Latvia, Lithuania, and Estonia; also available in [Latvian](https://eu-peers.eu/baltics-lv), [Lithuanian](https://eu-peers.eu/baltics-lt), and [Estonian](https://eu-peers.eu/baltics-ee)
- [French Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/french-community-of-practice) — also available in [French](https://eu-peers.eu/france-fr)
- [Transnational Community of Practice](https://eu-peers.eu/transnational) — cross-border peer exchange

## CMS-driven content collections

The site is built on Webflow and uses CMS collections for dynamic content:
- **News** — ~77 articles covering project updates, policy developments, and OSS-related topics
- **Events** — 30+ events per year across consortium partners and national communities
- **Showcases** — ~27 documented OSS cases across Europe, tagged by country, topic, and campaign
- **Members** — 229+ organisations in the community directory
- **Knowledge Assets** — 122+ resources in the Knowledge Hub, categorised by type and topic
- **Learning Lab sessions** — monthly thematic workshops with post-session reports
- **FAQs** — structured question-and-answer content on renovation topics
- **Glossary** — sector-specific terminology

### Learning Lab sessions (monthly thematic workshops)

Each session is led by a different consortium partner and produces a post-session report with practitioner insights.

- [Quality in Renovation Works](https://eu-peers.eu/june-2025-quality-in-renovation-works-filao-labs) — June 2025, led by Filao Labs. Covers quality assurance frameworks, contractor management, and works verification in OSS-coordinated renovations.
- [Energy Poverty](https://eu-peers.eu/september-2025-energy-poverty-climate-alliance) — September 2025, led by Climate Alliance. Addresses how One-Stop Shops can reach energy-poor households and integrate social objectives into renovation programmes.
- [Multilevel Governance in OSS Deployment](https://eu-peers.eu/october-2025-the-multilevel-governance-dimension-in-the-one-stop-shop-deployment-region-piemonte) — October 2025, led by Region Piemonte. Examines the coordination between municipal, regional, and national levels in establishing and scaling One-Stop Shops.
- [Monitoring the Impact of One-Stop Shops](https://eu-peers.eu/november-2025-monitoring-the-impact-of-one-stop-shops-enea) — November 2025, led by ENEA. Covers indicators, data collection methods, and reporting frameworks for measuring OSS effectiveness.
- [Homeowner Activation](https://eu-peers.eu/december-2025-home-owner-activation-tki) — December 2025, led by TKI. Strategies for engaging homeowners, overcoming renovation decision barriers, and sustaining participation through the renovation journey.
- [Alliances and Partnerships for One-Stop Shops](https://eu-peers.eu/january-2026-alliances-and-partnerships-for-one-stop-shops-gbce) — January 2026, led by GBCe. How OSS operators build effective partnerships with local authorities, financial institutions, and supply chain actors.
- [Business Models for One-Stop Shops](https://eu-peers.eu/february-2026-exploring-business-models-for-one-stop-shops-public-private-and-hybrid-approaches-gne) — February 2026, led by GNE Finance. Comparative analysis of public, private, and hybrid OSS business models, including revenue structures and sustainability.

### Key standalone resources

- [How to Set Up a One-Stop Shop](https://eu-peers.eu/how-to-setup-an-one-stop-shop) — practical step-by-step guidance for establishing an OSS, drawing on consortium experience and practitioner input
- [OSS Business Models — Learning Lab Report](https://eu-peers.eu/february-2026-exploring-business-models-for-one-stop-shops-public-private-and-hybrid-approaches-gne) — detailed report on public, private, and hybrid business models
- [Glossary](https://eu-peers.eu/glossary) — sector-specific terminology for One-Stop Shops, integrated home renovation services, and related policy concepts
- [Knowledge Hub](https://eu-peers.eu/knowledge-hub) — 122+ curated resources including guides, research papers, policy documents, and tools relevant to OSS practitioners

## Research and original data

EU PEERS produces original research and data, including:
- Pan-European survey of One-Stop Shop practitioners — operational data on governance models, financing approaches, service scope, staffing, and homeowner engagement across European OSS. First survey collected ~76 valid responses across 69 data fields; second survey in progress with improved methodology.
- Learning Lab reports — monthly practitioner-driven synthesis covering business models, monitoring, energy poverty, partnerships, homeowner activation, quality assurance, and multilevel governance (see individual session links above)
- A three-tier instrument maturity framework and eight OSS-suitability criteria for renovation financing instruments — published at EEDAL 2024, co-authored by Nicholas Stancioff, Karine Zarins, and Kirils Goncarovs
- Country-level data on OSS operating environments across consortium countries (France, Belgium, Germany, Spain, Latvia, Lithuania, Estonia, Italy, Hungary, Ireland)
- Train the Trainer programme — structured five-module training curriculum for OSS practitioners, covering the knowledge base needed to operate and scale One-Stop Shops

## Project context

- **Full name:** European Practitioners for Integrated Home Renovation Services
- **Acronym:** EU PEERS
- **Funding:** EU LIFE Programme (Clean Energy Transition sub-programme)
- **Grant agreement:** LIFE22-CET-EU-Peers / 101120790
- **Duration:** 2023–2027
- **Coordinator:** Climate Alliance (Klima-Bündnis), Frankfurt, Germany
- **Consortium:** 15 partners across 10 European countries
- **Website:** https://eu-peers.eu
- **Community platform:** HumHub (restricted to members)

### Consortium partners (confirmed from project activities)

- **Climate Alliance** (Klima-Bündnis) — Germany. Project coordinator. Led the Energy Poverty Learning Lab.
- **Filao Labs** — France. Led the Quality in Renovation Works Learning Lab. Active in renovation financing training.
- **ENEA** — Italy. National energy agency. Led the Monitoring Learning Lab.
- **Region Piemonte** — Italy. Led the Multilevel Governance Learning Lab.
- **GNE Finance** — Led the Business Models Learning Lab. Contributed research on OSS financing and business model analysis.
- **GBCe** (Green Building Council España) — Spain. Led the Alliances and Partnerships Learning Lab.
- **TKI** — Led the Homeowner Activation Learning Lab.
- Additional consortium partners contribute to national Communities of Practice across Ireland, Hungary, the Baltic States (Latvia, Lithuania, Estonia), and other countries.

## Languages

Primary language: English. The site provides localised pages in French, Spanish, Italian, Hungarian, Latvian, Lithuanian, Estonian, and German for key sections (About, News & Events, Community, Knowledge Hub, FAQs, legal pages).

## Contact

- General enquiries: https://eu-peers.eu/contact-us
- Membership: https://eu-peers.eu/membership-application
